title: The Fifth Sunday in Lent
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
April 8, 2019 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
This is the Fifth Sunday in Lent, also known as Passion Sunday, falling two weeks before Easter Sunday (April 21, 2019). Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would include John 11:1-45 (the raising of Lazarus) as the Gospel, with Ezekiel 37:1-14 (the valley of dry bones) and Romans 6:9-11 as supporting texts. This Sunday marks the deepening of Lenten focus on Christ's passion and death, with the raising of Lazarus foreshadowing Christ's own resurrection.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS
The Episcopal Church was in the midst of ongoing tensions following the 2018 General Convention's decision to remove traditional marriage language restrictions, a decision that continued to generate debate in conservative dioceses. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Robert Ethiope O'Neill, was navigating these broader church tensions while maintaining its pastoral presence in the Denver metropolitan area. No major diocesan-specific controversies or events were documented in the immediate week prior to this sermon.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS
Denver experienced unseasonably mild April weather that week, with temperatures in the 60s-70s Fahrenheit, typical for early spring. Governor Jared Polis's administration was in its early months (he took office in January 2019), focusing on education funding and transportation issues as legislative priorities. The Cathedral, located on Logan Street in downtown Denver's Capitol Hill neighborhood, served a congregation navigating the neighborhood's ongoing gentrification and demographic shifts.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)
The Mueller Report on Russian interference in the 2016 election was released on April 18, 2019 (10 days after this sermon), but investigations and speculation dominated the week of April 8. The 2020 presidential campaign was accelerating, with early primary contests drawing media attention. The Sudanese government had just experienced a military coup on April 11, 2019, but the immediate preceding week focused on domestic political developments and ongoing immigration debates.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS
Political turmoil in Venezuela continued under Nicolás Maduro, with ongoing humanitarian crisis and international intervention discussions. The UK remained mired in Brexit negotiations, with Theresa May's government struggling to secure Parliamentary approval for withdrawal agreements. The Notre-Dame Cathedral fire in Paris had occurred on April 15-16, 2019 (one week after this sermon date), so this was not yet a factor in this specific moment.

CULTURAL MOMENT
American culture in early April 2019 remained divided along partisan lines with high political polarization preceding the Mueller Report release. The final season of HBO's "Game of Thrones" was in production (premiering April 14), generating significant cultural conversation. Denver's sports consciousness was moderate—the Broncos offseason was underway, and the Avalanche and Rockies were in early season play, but April is generally a slower sports moment before the NBA and NHL playoffs accelerate.
